# Copilot instructions

## Project overview

- This is an Astro content-driven blog/site with React islands enabled in [astro.config.mjs](astro.config.mjs) (`@astrojs/react`).
- Core stack is Astro + React + GSAP (see [package.json](package.json)).
- Main routes are under [src/pages/](src/pages/); shared page shell is [src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro](src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro).
- Blog posts are markdown files in [src/blog/](src/blog/) validated by the `blog` collection schema in [src/content.config.ts](src/content.config.ts).
- Post pages are generated by [src/pages/posts/[...slug].astro](src/pages/posts/[...slug].astro) via `getCollection("blog")` and `render(post)`.
- Tag listing and tag detail routes are [src/pages/tags/index.astro](src/pages/tags/index.astro) and [src/pages/tags/[tag].astro](src/pages/tags/[tag].astro), both derived from `post.data.tags`.
- React UI islands can be used where needed; keep island behavior explicit and consistent with existing usage (for example [src/components/Greeting.jsx](src/components/Greeting.jsx)).

## Content and routing patterns

- New posts must be added in [src/blog/](src/blog/), not in [src/pages/](src/pages/).
- Required post frontmatter fields are `title`, `pubDate`, `description`, `author`, `image` (`url`, `alt`), and `tags` per [src/content.config.ts](src/content.config.ts).
- Markdown post rendering must go through [src/layouts/MarkDownPostLayout.astro](src/layouts/MarkDownPostLayout.astro) to keep author/date/tags/image metadata consistent.
- Blog index uses [src/components/BlogPost.astro](src/components/BlogPost.astro) links with URL shape `/posts/{post.id}/`.

## UI and interaction patterns

- Global styling and responsive nav behavior are centralized in [src/styles/global.css](src/styles/global.css); prefer extending existing `.site-nav`, `.menu`, `.nav-links` patterns.
- Header interaction is split across [src/components/Header.astro](src/components/Header.astro), [src/components/Menu.astro](src/components/Menu.astro), and [src/scripts/menu.js](src/scripts/menu.js).
- Theme switching is component + script based: [src/components/ThemeIcon.astro](src/components/ThemeIcon.astro) imports [src/scripts/themeIcon.js](src/scripts/themeIcon.js), which toggles the `dark` class and uses View Transitions when available.
- Mascot animations are SVG-first and GSAP-driven:
	- Homepage mascot: [src/components/CuLooMascot/CuLooMascot.astro](src/components/CuLooMascot/CuLooMascot.astro) + [src/scripts/culooMascot.js](src/scripts/culooMascot.js) (variant registry, weighted random selection, state machine, baseline reset).
	- 404 mascot: [src/components/CuLooMascot/CuLoo404Mascot.astro](src/components/CuLooMascot/CuLoo404Mascot.astro) + [src/scripts/culoo404Mascot.js](src/scripts/culoo404Mascot.js).
- When changing mascot SVG or animation scripts, keep class names and DOM structure aligned with selector usage in script files to avoid broken timelines.
- For complex SVG 3D rotation effects, preserve the current transform strategy used in scripts (including perspective-aware transform updates) unless a change is explicitly required.

## Consistency rules

- Match the nearest existing file style before editing: spacing, quote style, naming, and comment density.
- Prefer editing/extending existing pages and components over creating parallel duplicates.
- If a new file is necessary, wire it into existing routes/navigation/layouts to avoid orphaned files.
- Do not mass-reformat unrelated files; keep localized diffs and preserve existing conventions in each file type (`.astro`, `.js`, `.jsx`, `.md`, `.css`).
- In animation scripts, keep timeline logic readable and state-safe: avoid introducing overlapping tweens that bypass existing reset/lock flow.

## Dev and deployment workflows

- Local dev: `npm run dev`; build: `npm run build`; preview: `npm run preview` (see [package.json](package.json)).
- GitHub Pages deploy is defined in [.github/workflows/deploy.yml](.github/workflows/deploy.yml): `npm ci` -> `npm run build` -> upload `dist` -> `deploy-pages`.
- For deploy failures, use GitHub Actions logs first; common breakpoints are dependency install and Astro build.

## Communication rules

- When the user asks for an explanation, assume they are new to the topic by default.
- If technical terms are used, add brief plain-language explanations for those terms.
